title: Day 5 — Command-execution permission gate: PermissionRequest → decide_permission → bash preview

ledger.delta: messages += user('Use bash to run pytest --version'); permission_mode=default

frames.caption: The CLI parses the prompt and --permission-mode (default is 'default'). run_agent threads permission_mode='default' through the loop closure — today every tool call has to flow through this mode flag.

The model sees the new bash tool registered at the end of default_tools and decides to call it. The input_schema exposes only command / timeout / background — the dangerous-command list, user confirmation, and three-mode permission engine stay invisible to the model.

gate.rule: Day 5 signature: PermissionRequest(bash) → decide_permission (_DANGEROUS_PATTERNS + _READONLY_TOOLS + mode triage) → ask → Command preview + confirm_command y/N

frames.caption: agent.py wraps the tool_call into PermissionRequest(tool_name='bash', args={...}, mode='default', cwd=...) and hands it to decide_permission. bash is not in _READONLY_TOOLS, not in _ASK_TOOLS, the mode is neither plan nor acceptEdits; _is_dangerous('pytest --version') matches none of the regex patterns; the function returns PermissionDecision('ask'). The ask branch's bash arm prints Command: pytest --version (yellow) plus timeout/cwd (dim), then confirm_command pops 'Run this command? [y/N]'. This is Day 5's signature frame — v1's if/elif chain in agent.py gets replaced by this one decide_permission call in v2.

ledger.delta: subprocess.run('pytest --version', shell=True, cwd=ctx.cwd, env=_MINIMAL_ENV, timeout=30) → stdout='pytest 8.x.x'; truncate_output(max_chars=12000)

frames.caption: The user presses y, control returns to tools.run(bash) → bash_runner.run_sync. subprocess.run uses shell=True so the model writes commands the same way you'd type them in a terminal; cwd is pinned to ctx.cwd (not the host's CWD); env is restricted to _MINIMAL_ENV which only forwards PATH/HOME/USER/SHELL so secret env vars never leak into the child process. stdout is truncated to 12000 chars; returncode=0 so the content is returned verbatim.

Same shape as Day 4: the assistant's tool_use is appended as one message, then this round's tool_result blocks land inside one user message. Today's interceptor streams 'Command: ...', 'Run this command?', and 'observation: pytest 8.x.x' interleaved with emit so the terminal reader sees tool_call → command preview → user types y → observation in real wall-clock order.

card.label: final: pytest 8.x.x is installed in this environment.

frames.caption: Second request. The model reads 'pytest 8.x.x' from the tool_result, decides the command succeeded, returns plain text with stop_reason=end_turn. for step in range(max_steps) exits on this frame.

modelResponse.text: pytest 8.x.x is installed in this environment.

card.label: stdout ← 'final: pytest 8.x.x is installed in this environment.'

frames.caption: emit has already streamed tool_call / Command preview / observation / final to the terminal in wall-clock order. If the same prompt had used 'git push --force origin main' instead, frame 3 would route to the deny path — _is_dangerous matches the regex, PermissionDecision('deny') returns 'Dangerous command blocked', and no Command preview or confirm prompt ever appears.

modelResponse.text: pytest 8.x.x is installed in this environment.
